judge_details.reasoning: The Sunday dialogue is a repetitive victory lap that recycles the same 'restraint/didn't overthink it' theme four times without adding anything new. Characters sound nearly identical in register and sentiment, and there's zero tension or conflict to drive the scene — it's just mutual congratulation with slight paraphrasing. The specificity that made earlier days strong (60-40 ratio, steam absorption, shot two's crisp exterior) is almost entirely absent.
strengths: Callbacks to prior week's themes show continuity awareness
Margaret's exit line 'Going home now. Good week.' has a naturalistic abruptness that fits her character
issues: 'Restraint/didn't overthink it' sentiment is repeated nearly verbatim by three different characters with no variation or pushback
No character-specific voice distinction — Marcus, Steph, and Margaret are interchangeable in tone and content here
Zero conflict or friction makes the scene dramatically inert
No concrete recipe details anchor the dialogue to the actual work product
Devon Park and Julian Torres are absent with no acknowledgment, breaking ensemble continuity
